_meta.purpose: Image-edit pass — within-tier from Hussar Conscript (L4) to Hussar (L5). Take the L4 composited cavalry lancer (Tier 2 entry: spear iron banding, scale-mail short sleeves, both shoulders pauldroned, studded leather peytral on horse, chest-strap rivet plate) and produce the L5 'Hussar' variant.
history: v1 (2026-05-06): initial author — Hussar (Tier 2 mid) within-tier with 5 adds: (1) partial steel breastplate covering upper chest only with livery still dominant below, (2) steel vambraces wrapping both forearms wrist-to-mid-forearm, (3) small steel chamfron forehead plate on the horse, (4) additional steel reinforcement plate on the spear haft beyond the L4 iron rings, (5) small steel bevor/collar plate at the rider's throat. Locked composite thresholds 15/35 pool=12 (no tier-break dilate at L5 — within-tier). Spatial-geometry directional language only (SPEAR-SIDE/REIN-SIDE).

v2 (2026-05-07): revised per AD FeedbackV0. (a) Scale-mail sleeves now extend from short (mid-bicep, L4) to FULL bicep-to-wrist coverage at L5 per AD 'the chainmail being sleeveless feels odd. For the later levels, I would extend it to fully cover the arms.' Vambraces (#2) sit ON TOP of the new full-arm scale mail at the forearm. The brown leather under-jacket sleeves are now hidden under the scale mail at the upper arm; only a thin band of leather may show at the inner armpit. (b) REMOVED the L5 'spear haft reinforcement plate' positive add (per Detail Economy rule — surface ornament with low thumbnail readability; the L4 iron banding rings + reinforced socket already give the haft its banded read). Visible adds count remains 5: (1) full-arm scale-mail sleeves, (2) partial breastplate, (3) vambraces (on top of scale mail), (4) small chamfron, (5) small bevor/collar. Also: knife sheath references removed from preserve block; white-blaze references stripped.
v3 (2026-05-07): pipeline update — drop L1_Base scale anchor from image_urls and prompt body. Single-image input (prior-level composited keeper only) per updated pipeline + docs.
v4 (2026-05-07): scrub belt-studs PRESERVE/negative references — L2 IRON STUDS beat dropped per AD detail-economy (drift-corrected pass).
v6 (2026-05-07): added prophylactic anti-rivet/stud guardrail negative — same as Infantry chain, applied through the Cavalry chain after rivet bleed-through observed at L3 generation.

v8 (2026-05-07): retone all metal-color language from cool silver-gray to warm silver-gray matching the rendered L1 kettle-helm tone — cool/blueish steel was reading distinctly different from the L1 baseline's warm gray, getting more pronounced at higher levels. Added negative clause locking out cool/blue-gray steel palette.

v9 (2026-05-07): rename L5 'chest band' (below the partial breastplate) to 'skirt band' for terminology consistency with L6+. The strip is below the breastplate, so 'skirt band' is the geometrically accurate term — this is a no-op visual change, just naming.
v10 (2026-05-07): retone metal language from 'warm gray' -> 'neutral gray' (prior pass overshot warm; balanced gray with neither blue nor brown shadow tint matches the L1 baseline most accurately).

v11 (2026-05-07): scrub model-invisible context from prompt body — removed L<n> level-tag references (model is stateless and cannot resolve our level naming), authoring-decision parentheticals (LOCKED/REVISED/REMOVED dates, per AD FeedbackV0, etc.), and cross-level range refs. All persistent decisions remain in _meta.history + the progression plan + CLAUDE.md. See Archer/CLAUDE.md § Part 3 locked decision #27 (drift-corrected: residual parenthetical level-tags also dropped).

adds.title: PARTIAL STEEL BREASTPLATE
adds.summary: UPPER CHEST ONLY — add a curved steel breastplate covering the rider's UPPER CHEST from the collarbone down to roughly the bottom of the sternum (approximately the upper third of the torso, ending well above the natural waist where the leather belt sits).

adds.title: STEEL VAMBRACES ON BOTH FOREARMS LAYERED ON TOP OF THE SCALE-MAIL SLEEVES

adds.title: SMALL STEEL CHAMFRON FOREHEAD PLATE ON THE HORSE
adds.summary: add a small neutral silver-gray steel matching the L1 kettle-helm tone (balanced gray, neither blue-shadowed nor brown-shadowed) plate covering the horse's FOREHEAD from the poll (between the ears) down to the area between the eyes.

adds.title: SMALL STEEL BEVOR / COLLAR PLATE AT THE RIDER'S THROAT

image_urls: <<UPLOAD: UnitProgression/Cavalry/out/v1/L4/composite/cavalry_L4_v3_composited.png>> (content reference — apply the upgrades onto this character)
prompt: EDIT GOAL — Apply FIVE visible additive upgrades to the mounted cavalry lancer in the FIRST input image, marking the within-tier promotion from Hussar Conscript to Hussar. The rider already wears the existing Hussar Conscript kit: kettle-helm, mustache (no beard, clean-shaven cheeks/chin), yellow+red split surcoat over brown leather long-sleeve under-jacket, scale-mail SHORT sleeves on both upper arms shoulder-to-mid-bicep (these EXTEND to full bicep-to-wrist coverage at feedback — see Edit Goal item 1), two matching steel pauldron discs on both shoulders, leather waist belt with plain leather belt flanking the brass buckle, leather grip cord wrapped around the upper third of the wooden spear haft with iron banding rings (3) and a reinforced steel socket at the iron leaf-tip seam, iron pommel cap at the spear haft butt, hip pouch on the spear-side hip (only belt accessory — knife sheath was removed at), iron toe-cap strip on the front of each boot, blue trousers, brown leather knee-high riding boots in stirrups. The horse already wears the existing Hussar Conscript kit: iron-buckled bridle with iron browband, iron banding on saddle pommel and cantle, studded leather peytral on the chest beneath the red+blue checkered caparison. CRITICAL SPATIAL ANCHOR — the upright wooden SPEAR HAFT is the most prominent vertical line on one side of the rider, with the iron leaf-tip near the top of the canvas. Treat 'spear-side' as the side of the rider's body / canvas where the upright wooden spear haft is visible (the side closer to the horse's tail), and 'rein-side' as the OPPOSITE side where the rein hand grips the reins, the side closest to the horse's head and mane. Throughout this prompt, do NOT use 'right' or 'left' to interpret placement; use SPEAR-SIDE / REIN-SIDE only. (1) FULL-ARM SCALE-MAIL SLEEVES (extend the existing short sleeves to bicep-to-wrist coverage) — extend the existing scale-mail short sleeves DOWNWARD from mid-bicep (where they currently end) all the way past the elbow to the wrist, fully covering both arms in chunky overlapping rounded scale-mail (neutral silver-gray steel matching the existing kettle-helm tone (balanced gray, neither blue-shadowed nor brown-shadowed), scales arranged in horizontal rows with each row overlapping the row below by half a scale, soft top-down highlight on each scale and warm-brown shadow under the lower edge of each scale). The scale-mail sleeves now cover the entire upper arm AND forearm. The brown leather long-sleeve under-jacket is NOW HIDDEN UNDER THE FULL-ARM SCALE MAIL at the upper arm and forearm — only a thin band of leather may show at the inner armpit. NO exposed leather-jacket gap on the bicep, the elbow, or the forearm. NOT chainmail / fine ring mesh / interlocking rings — chunky overlapping rounded scale plates per the existing spec. (2) PARTIAL STEEL BREASTPLATE — UPPER CHEST ONLY — add a curved steel breastplate covering the rider's UPPER CHEST from the collarbone down to roughly the bottom of the sternum (approximately the upper third of the torso, ending well above the natural waist where the leather belt sits). Smooth neutral silver-gray steel matching the existing kettle-helm tone (balanced gray, neither blue-shadowed nor brown-shadowed) surface with a slight medial ridge (a soft raised vertical line down the center of the breastplate from the throat to the lower edge). The lower edge of the breastplate is rounded with a slight outward flare and one row of small dark-iron rivets along the rim. The breastplate sits OVER the surcoat at the upper chest. CRITICAL — THE LIVERY SURCOAT MUST REMAIN VISIBLY DOMINANT ON THE LOWER CHEST AND ABDOMEN BELOW THE BREASTPLATE. The mustard-yellow + crimson-red split surcoat panels are still fully visible from the breastplate's bottom edge down to the leather waist belt — at the livery now reads as a SKIRT BAND between the breastplate (above) and the wider belt (below), with both yellow and red panels still clearly distinguishable, NOT shrunk into a thin strip, NOT covered by the breastplate. The breastplate covers ONLY the upper third of the torso. (3) STEEL VAMBRACES ON BOTH FOREARMS LAYERED ON TOP OF THE SCALE-MAIL SLEEVES — add neutral silver-gray steel matching the existing kettle-helm tone (balanced gray, neither blue-shadowed nor brown-shadowed) cuff plates wrapping both of the rider's FOREARMS from the wrist up to mid-forearm. Each vambrace is approximately 4 inches tall, smooth painted steel with two visible round dark-iron rivets per cuff at the seam edge. CRITICAL — the new vambraces sit ON TOP of the new full-arm scale-mail sleeves at the forearm (the scale-mail covers the entire arm bicep-to-wrist; the vambrace is layered over the scale at the forearm, NOT replacing it). The scale-mail is visible BENEATH the vambraces above the elbow and remains visible at the upper arm. Both forearms get matching vambraces symmetrically. The bare hands (warm peachy-tan skin) remain visible below the vambrace cuffs at the wrist. (4) SMALL STEEL CHAMFRON FOREHEAD PLATE ON THE HORSE — add a small neutral silver-gray steel matching the existing kettle-helm tone (balanced gray, neither blue-shadowed nor brown-shadowed) plate covering the horse's FOREHEAD from the poll (between the ears) down to the area between the eyes. The chamfron is approximately a small rounded shield-shape (about 4 inches wide, 5 inches tall), with small ear-cutouts at the top so the horse's ears poke through unobstructed, and a riveted seam edge. The chamfron sits OVER the leather bridle's headstall but does NOT extend over the horse's muzzle, eyes, or noseband — the muzzle and eyes remain visible solid chestnut brown below the chamfron's lower edge. The chamfron has a slight central medial ridge or raised boss, with two visible round dark-iron rivets at the lower seam where it meets the leather bridle. Cool silver-gray steel matching the rider's vambraces and breastplate. (5) SMALL STEEL BEVOR / COLLAR PLATE AT THE RIDER'S THROAT — add a short neutral silver-gray steel matching the existing kettle-helm tone (balanced gray, neither blue-shadowed nor brown-shadowed) half-collar plate covering the FRONT of the rider's throat from the collarbone (where the upper edge of the new breastplate begins) up to the lower jaw line. The bevor is approximately 2 inches tall, curved to follow the neck contour, smooth painted steel with one visible round dark-iron rivet at each end of the lower seam. The bevor wraps the front and side of the throat but leaves the back of the neck open. The mustache and lower face remain fully visible above the bevor's upper edge. The bevor adds visible volume to the chest silhouette (silhouette mightiness — the throat now reads as armored). ===== PRESERVE FROM THE INPUT IMAGE EXACTLY ===== every other element of the FIRST input image must remain identical in shape, color, position, and proportion. THE RIDER FACE: chiseled chibi jawline; confident flat closed-mouth expression; thick dark-brown eyebrows with a slight stern furrow; large warm steel-blue eyes with a single bright catchlight in each iris; prominent slightly-hooked nose; warm peachy-tan skin tone with soft rosy blush; THICK DARK-BROWN HANDLEBAR MUSTACHE handlebar-shaped covering the upper lip and curving down past the corners of the mouth — handlebar shape, NOT bushy, NOT walrus, NOT extending into a beard; cheeks and chin CLEAN-SHAVEN with NO beard, NO stubble, NO five o'clock shadow; dark-brown short hair visible only as a thin fringe at the forehead under the front edge of the kettle-helm. THE RIDER HEAD COVERING: the steel kettle-helm — neutral silver-gray steel matching the existing kettle-helm tone (balanced gray, neither blue-shadowed nor brown-shadowed) domed helm with a forward-projecting riveted brim, two visible round iron rivets on each side at the brim seam, gentle sloping forehead curve, brim projecting forward over the eyes, open face. Smooth painted steel — NOT scale, NOT chainmail. THE RIDER TORSO: the sleeveless mustard-yellow and crimson-red split SURCOAT — viewer-LEFT-of-center half mustard-yellow, viewer-RIGHT-of-center half crimson-red, vertical seam down the chest center, simple round neckline, hem reaches the natural waist — at the surcoat is OVERLAPPED at its UPPER CHEST PORTION by the new breastplate (Edit Goal item 1), but the surcoat remains FULLY VISIBLE from the breastplate's bottom edge down to the waist belt as a clearly readable yellow+red split SKIRT BAND with both panels distinguishable (NOT shrunk into a thin strip, NOT covered, NOT erased). The wide brown leather waist belt with brass buckle centered at the front. THE RIDER ARMS AND HANDS: full-arm scale-mail sleeves now cover both arms bicep-to-wrist (Edit Goal item 1 — extending the existing short sleeves downward); the brown leather long-sleeve under-jacket is now HIDDEN UNDER the scale-mail at the upper arm and forearm (only a thin band of leather may show at the inner armpit); the new steel vambraces on both forearms wrist-to-mid-forearm sit ON TOP of the scale-mail at the forearm (Edit Goal item 3); both bare hands warm peachy-tan skin (the spear-side hand grips the wooden spear haft at the existing grip cord; the rein-side hand grips the brown leather reins at the horse's withers). Both shoulders have matching steel pauldron discs (unchanged). THE RIDER LEGS: dark blue / muted steel-blue knee-length trousers slightly loose at the cuff (visible under the saddle skirt on the spear-side); chunky warm-brown leather knee-high riding boots reaching mid-calf, both boots in stirrups. THE WEAPON: the long wooden spear with iron leaf-tip — heavy warm-brown wooden haft running approximately six feet long, neutral gray (matching the existing kettle-helm tone, balanced gray) iron leaf-shaped spearhead at the top, held vertically in the rider's spear-side hand at shoulder height with the spear-tip pointing UP toward the top of the canvas. The iron pommel cap at the haft butt remains unchanged. The leather grip cord wraps the haft around the gripping hand area, unchanged. The iron banding rings (3) and reinforced socket at the leaf-tip seam are unchanged. The wooden haft remains warm-brown wood between the rings — NO additional reinforcement plate is added at this level (removed). THE REINS: the brown leather reins running from the horse's bit back to the rider's rein-side hand at the horse's withers, gripped relaxed. THE HORSE: the SOLID chestnut-brown chibi horse facing the viewer's RIGHT with head and muzzle on the rein-side of the frame and rump and tail on the spear-side of the frame, standing four-square with all four hooves visible at the bottom of the frame, neck arched, ears forward, single dark eye visible on the rein-side of the head with a bright catchlight. THE HORSE IS SOLID CHESTNUT BROWN ACROSS THE ENTIRE BODY — NO WHITE MARKINGS — no white blaze, no white star, no white socks, no white speckling. Dark brown mane along the top of the neck approximately three inches long, dark brown tail extending behind the rump. The horse's pose, anatomy, solid-brown color, mane, tail, ears, and eye all remain identical in shape, color, and position to the input image. THE HORSE TACK: the brown leather saddle with the EXISTING iron banding on pommel and cantle (unchanged); the brown leather bridle with the EXISTING iron browband and iron square buckles on cheekstrap and throatlash (unchanged); the EXISTING studded leather peytral on the horse's chest sitting BENEATH the caparison (unchanged); the new steel chamfron forehead plate per Edit Goal item (3) sits OVER the bridle's headstall on the horse's forehead. THE CAPARISON: the large rectangular cloth caparison with red+blue checkered pattern (alternating crimson-red and steel-blue squares) and steel-blue trim band along the lower edge with shallow rectangular tabs — UNCHANGED, identical to the input image. THE EXISTING the levels above KIT: the eight dark-iron belt studs flanking the brass buckle; the iron pommel cap at the spear haft butt; the iron browband and iron square buckles on the bridle; the hip pouch on the spear-side hip; the leather grip cord wrap on the spear haft; the iron banding on the saddle pommel and cantle; the single steel pauldron disc on the rein-side shoulder; the second matching steel pauldron disc on the spear-side shoulder; the iron banding rings + reinforced socket on the spear haft; the studded leather peytral on the horse's chest; the iron toe-cap strip on the front of each riding boot — ALL preserved unchanged. (The short scale-mail sleeves are EXTENDED at to full bicep-to-wrist coverage per Edit Goal item 1 — the scale-mail itself is preserved and grown, not replaced.) ===== POSE (must match the input image exactly) ===== Three-quarter view, the rider's body angled approximately fifteen to twenty-five degrees toward the viewer's left so the rider's body presents to camera. The HORSE faces toward the viewer's RIGHT. Rider sits upright in the saddle with both feet in stirrups, slight forward lean. THE SPEAR ARM (the rider's arm on the spear-side of the frame) is bent at the elbow with the upper arm vertical and the forearm angled slightly forward; the hand grips the wooden spear haft at the existing grip cord wrap; the spear stands roughly VERTICAL with the iron leaf-tip pointing UP toward the top of the canvas. THE REIN ARM (the rider's arm on the rein-side of the frame) is extended forward and slightly down, elbow bent at roughly 110 degrees; the hand grips the brown leather reins at the level of the horse's withers / shoulder. The pose reads as a calm mounted patrol stance. NOT a charge, NOT a couched-lance attack, NOT a rear, NOT a pivot, NOT a walk, NOT a gallop. ===== STYLE / OUTLINE ===== Supercell / Kingshot stylized mobile game art, hand-painted digital illustration with soft painted shading and visible brushwork, warm subsurface skin, saturated rich colors, warm shadow tones never gray, soft painted highlights on metal and leather. Clean thin black silhouette outline around the entire rider+horse+spear+caparison silhouette in deep warm brown-black. Interior linework remains soft and value-driven, never hard black. ===== FRAMING / SCALE (must match these canonical numbers exactly) ===== 1024x1024 square canvas. Rider+horse silhouette occupies approximately 77.6% of canvas vertical height (~795px tall) and approximately 60.6% of canvas horizontal width (~621px wide). TOP of rider's helm at approximately 11.4% from top of canvas (~117px headroom). BOTTOM of horse's hooves at approximately 10.9% from bottom of canvas (~112px below). Rider+horse vertically centered. The wooden spear haft extends UP past the helm with the iron leaf-tip near or at the canvas top edge. DO NOT zoom in, DO NOT shrink, DO NOT add wider borders, DO NOT crop the helm or hooves, DO NOT shift to bottom edge. Clean opaque WHITE background with no environmental context, no light gray tint, no off-white shading, minimal contact shadow under the horse's hooves only, 1:1 square aspect ratio. ===== NEGATIVE — DO NOT ===== DO NOT extend the steel breastplate downward to cover the abdomen or waist — at the breastplate covers ONLY the upper third of the torso; the surcoat livery (yellow + red split panels) must remain visibly DOMINANT below the breastplate from its lower edge to the waist belt; DO NOT shrink the surcoat livery into a thin strip; DO NOT cover, occlude, or remove the surcoat livery panels below the breastplate; DO NOT replace the surcoat with a body coat, gambeson, robe, or full breastplate at this level (full breastplate arrives at this level); DO NOT add full plate, full cuirass, full body armor, or any armor that covers the abdomen at this level; DO NOT add a closed helm, sallet, half-visor, full visor, helm crest, helm horns, or any structural addition to the kettle-helm — the helm stays exactly as the existing kettle-helm at this level; DO NOT remove the kettle-helm or replace it with a different helm; DO NOT add iron rivets, studs, embossed dots, or any small metal punctuation along the leather waist belt, the leather under-jacket sleeves, the surcoat hem, the saddle leather, the bridle straps, the caparison panels, the rider's hands, or any surface beyond what the input image already shows; DO NOT add metal banding rings or riveted hardware appearing as decoration on the kettle-helm body, the surcoat, or the wooden spear haft middle (the existing kettle's existing brim rivets are functional construction rivets and stay unchanged); DO NOT render any new metal piece in cool blue-gray steel, bluish steel, icy silver, chrome, OR overly warm/brown-tinted steel — every new metal addition must match the NEUTRAL gray tone of the existing kettle-helm and spear leaf-tip (balanced gray, NEITHER blue-shadowed NOR brown-shadowed; soft painted highlights that read clean-white, neither cool nor warm); DO NOT change any facial feature; DO NOT grow the mustache into a beard or add stubble or a five o'clock shadow — cheeks and chin stay clean-shaven with NO beard at all; DO NOT change the surcoat colors, panel layout, neckline, or hem; DO NOT change the trouser color or boot color; DO NOT add metal sabatons, greaves, leg armor, tassets, or any leg metal at this level (sabatons + tassets arrive at this level); DO NOT swap the hand gripping the spear; DO NOT swap the hand gripping the reins; DO NOT change the spear shape, the wooden haft length, the iron leaf-tip shape or size; DO NOT replace the wooden haft with a steel lance haft (steel haft arrives at this level); DO NOT add a pennon flag or banner to the spear (that arrives at this level); DO NOT remove the existing leather grip cord wrap or the existing iron banding rings on the haft — both remain visible at with no new haft additions; DO NOT add gauntlets at this level (gauntlets arrive at this level); DO NOT remove the scale-mail short sleeves on the upper arms; DO NOT keep the existing short (mid-bicep) scale-mail sleeves at this level — the sleeves MUST extend to FULL bicep-to-wrist coverage; DO NOT leave any exposed leather-jacket gap on the bicep, the elbow, or the forearm — the full-arm scale-mail covers the upper arm AND forearm; DO NOT place the new vambraces UNDER the scale-mail — the vambraces sit ON TOP of the scale-mail at the forearm; DO NOT use chainmail / fine ring mesh / interlocking metal rings on the new full-arm sleeves — they are SCALE MAIL with chunky overlapping rounded plates; DO NOT add a spear-haft reinforcement plate, additional steel band, or new wider steel cuff on the wooden spear haft at this level — the existing iron banding rings + reinforced socket carry the haft's banded read; DO NOT add a knife sheath, dagger sheath, or any second belt accessory — the existing hip pouch is the only belt accessory through every level; DO NOT remove the existing pauldrons on either shoulder; DO NOT add a leather X-strap chest harness, baldric, sword belt, or any visible chest strap at this level — the new breastplate covers the upper chest where chest straps would sit; DO NOT add gold trim, gold studs, gold accents, gold rivets, gold band, or any gold anywhere — first gold appears at this level; DO NOT add gemstones, jewels, embroidery, runes, engraved patterns, or filigree; DO NOT extend the chamfron over the horse's muzzle, eyes, or noseband — the chamfron at covers ONLY the forehead from poll to between the eyes; the muzzle and eyes remain visible solid chestnut brown below the chamfron's lower edge; DO NOT add any white markings on the horse — no white blaze, no white star, no white socks, no white speckling — the horse is solid chestnut brown across the entire body; DO NOT add a crinet (neck plates), flanchards (flank plates), or any other horse barding beyond the chamfron and the existing peytral at this level (crinet arrives at this level, flanchards at this level); DO NOT remove the existing studded leather peytral on the horse's chest; DO NOT change the horse coat color from solid chestnut brown; DO NOT change the horse's pose; DO NOT make the horse rear, walk, gallop, pivot, or move; DO NOT change the horse to a pony, hobby-horse, wooden cutout horse, donkey, mule, or any other species; DO NOT change the horse's mane shape, tail shape, ear shape, or neck arch; DO NOT change the caparison's red+blue checkered pattern, the steel-blue trim band, or the tabbed lower edge; DO NOT add any heraldic emblem, cross, lion, eagle, or coat-of-arms to the caparison or surcoat at this level; DO NOT change the chibi proportions; DO NOT change the camera angle, framing, or composition; DO NOT crop the figure or the horse; DO NOT change the background, add scenery, or add a ground plane other than the existing minimal contact shadow; DO NOT make the background transparent, checkerboard-patterned, light-gray, off-white, or any non-white color; DO NOT drift the art style; DO NOT add a glow, rim light, blue rim light, cyan rim light, or any new light source; DO NOT change the aspect ratio from 1:1 square.
